items.title: Master I and II · Gender Studies

description.text: Critical and intersectional analysis of gender relations, discrimination and inclusion policies (LGBTQIA+, feminism, minorities). Research tools, project management, advocacy and social change.

items.title: Bac+2 · Web and Mobile Web Developer · Full-stack JavaScript

description.text:
**Frontend**: HTML5 · CSS3 · Sass/SCSS · JavaScript · TypeScript · React 
 **Backend**: Node.js · Express.js · PostgreSQL · Sequelize 
 **Specialisation**: React · Redux
**Principles and skills**: MVC Architecture · Active Record · Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) · Data Modelling (CDM, LDM) · Agile Project Management · Responsive Web Design · REST API
**Tools and methodologies**: Git · Insomnia, Postman · Shell · ESLint

items.title: Master II · Public Policy · International Humanitarian Action

description.text:
**Option**: Emergency and rehabilitation 
 Graduated with honours (AB)

Project cycle management, quality monitoring, evaluation and capitalisation 
 Country case studies, role-plays, project design, capacity building and partnerships

**Internship thesis I**: "*ICTs in the international solidarity sector, reflections on a forced integration*" 
 **Internship thesis II**: "*The notion of crisis and its use on migration issues, from a mobilisation tool to the misuse of an object turned political*"

International crisis management simulation, world cultural areas 
 Religions and globalisation, European political economy, comparative foreign policies
**Thesis**: "*The externalisation of migration controls in the Sahel. A study of a new tool of an extra-territorialised European border management policy*"
Specialisation in administrative law, international law and European law

Diploma awarded by Université Toulouse I · Capitole 
 Graduated with honours (B)
